issues.title: Invalid Unicode in TextEditingController's `text` Crashes the App on iOS
comments:

stable : 3.38.4
master : 3.40.0-1.0.pre-118

This seems to be an Objective-C crash, I suspect we should redirect this to the iOS team. I'll investigate and reroute if necessary.

Redirecting this to the iOS team. It seems the iOS embedder will need to be updated to be able to decode JSON strings that Dart escaped due to lone surrogates. It's possible the iOS embedder might have additional issues with these malformed strings even after we fix this JSON decoding..

I'm leaning towards the framework asserting when you get a malformed string instead of trying to handle unpaired surrogates (the framework is already doing that in some places, e.g., https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/c00f050f119ed59509b94b290d7447cf29be7a5e/packages/flutter/lib/src/painting/text_painter.dart#L190, because the framework isn't acting merely as an interface between the system services and the text field UI, some parts of the framework also consume the string). 

I think it should be up to the app developer to sanitize the user input, IIRC it's almost never desirable/intentional to have unpaired surrogates in your string (unless you're trying to break something). 

I agree that the crash shouldn't happen in production but there are so many services (for example the input method) that are going to be consuming the string and it's very difficult to guarantee that all of them can handle malformed strings so from that perspective we should fail faster. 

Also the [JSON spec](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8259#section-8.2) says the unpaired surrogate **can** be escaped but it's not required. I suspect the GTK and windows implementation will have the same problem to some degree, IIRC they use UTF8.

Thank you @LongCatIsLooong for the review and your input.

We’ve fixed the issue on our side by implementing a sanitizer in Dart and applying it as a TextField formatter. However, I believe this issue should also be addressed at the engine level, as the app should not crash in production.

We observed crashes in some user flows caused by a system channel call (JSON with a malformed string). We were unable to reproduce the issue as an end user, but we were able to reproduce it in code by manually providing a malformed string (half of a surrogate pair).

This suggests that other system channel calls may also receive malformed strings and potentially crash the app. In practice, we may never know the exact source of such input—it could come from a speech-to-text service, a custom keyboard, or another external provider.

As you mentioned, many services consume strings, and it’s difficult to guarantee that malformed input will never occur.

On Android, system channel calls are sanitized, so no crash occurs.
On iOS and macOS, the app crashes.

I haven’t been able to test this on Windows/Linux platforms yet.

I see that you reviewed https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/179775, it basically avoids crash by returning nil and skipping the system call. It might not be the optimal solution as it's discussed in the comments. 

But there is another PR (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/179978) introducing a sanitizer to fix the problem. Could you please review it and share your ideas on that?

Yeah the approach makes sense, however when unpaired surrogates are discarded and replaced with the replacement character in text fields, the framework will likely receive an update telling it that the string in the text field has changed, and the framework would usually invoke user callbacks like `TextField.onChanged` assuming the change is user input originated from the IME. 

This IMO is acceptable but not ideal, and in that case I think it's more important to let developers know that if you have malformed strings in a text field, it may not crash in production but the behavior is not defined so all bets are off.

A workaround on this issue is to replaced LisyView with Column and wrap the Column in a SingleChildScrollView.

Not just with the list view, it also bypass the Form validation if the field is not visible in CustomScrollView. And if we're using CustomScrollView and slivers then that cannot be replaced with Column and SingleChildScrollView to solve the issue.

The root cause on my issue was I was misusing provider. My model for ChangeNotifierProvider was too big. So updating a widget deep down in the widget tree caused rebuilding of pretty much all elements on the screen. Splitting it up helped

Any update on this issue? Our app has dynamically generated forms that can get quite large, so replacing `ListView` with `Column` and `SingleChildScrollView` means the validation works, but performance slows dramatically. The workaround now is to manually validate (as in, check all `TextEditingController`'s and display an error message if any of them are invalid).

I agree that my previous comment (`to replace LisyView by Column and wrap the Column in a SingleChildScrollView.`) does not cover all cases.

Same happens in PageView. Probably the same issue.

Using a keep alive is a good workaround for this. The Form itself does not know it is in a list potentially disposing of form fields that are out of view, and the list view does not know what its specific children are so it cannot decide what should or should not be kept alive. [This comment is helpful for achieving that](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/56159#issuecomment-791938191). The children will still be built on demand, but then kept alive so the list does not dispose of them.


We might be able to add some keep alive logic to the form widgets as an optional configuration though.. not sure.
It also would probably be good to see if we can surface some kind of error from validate if some of the children are missing.

Just chipping in to say I'm also encountering this bug, and it's a pain.
I'm building primarily Stateless so the mixin won't help me, and for some reason, the KeepAlive() widget also ain't working.

Gonna have to revert to figuring out columns and SingleChildScrollViews for the time being.
any help with this issue, has it been resolved yet?! i've tried using keep alive but still doesn't do the work
Any update ?

issues.title: Embedding flutter web app using an iframe causes zoom issues with keyboard input in IOS Safari
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Execute `flutter build web` on the code sample, host it, embed into an iframe, open in IOS Safari (or visit https://ios-iframe-test.web.app/test.html)
2. Tap on the text field.


**Expected results:** Zoom stays the same, the same as if you were opening the web app directly (see https://ios-iframe-test.web.app/).

**Actual results:** Safari zooms into the text field, like it would a web page input that was below 16px. 

<details>
<summary>Code sample</summary>


```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});
  
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: const MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyHomePage({super.key, required this.title});

  final String title;

  @override
  State<MyHomePage> createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  String _text = '';

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {

    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: Text(widget.title),
      ),
      body: Center(
        child: Column(

          mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
          children: <Widget>[
            const Text(
              'Below is your text:',
            ),
            Text(
              _text,
              style: Theme.of(context).textTheme.headline4,
            ),
            TextField(
              style: TextStyle(fontSize: 25),
              onChanged: (text){
                setState(() {
                  _text = text;
                });
                },
            )
          ],
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>

Replacing the viewport line to this in the `<head>` fixed the zoom issue for me

```html
<meta flt-viewport="" name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no">
```

This was a workaround by @ditman for an initial page loading zoom issue I had

issues.title: IME (Soft-Keyboard) not closing with back button on Amazon FireTV Stick
issues.body:
<!-- Thank you for using Flutter!

     If you are looking for support, please check out our documentation
     or consider asking a question on Stack Overflow:
      * https://flutter.dev/
      * https://api.flutter.dev/
      * https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/flutter?sort=frequent

     If you have found a bug or if our documentation doesn't have an answer
     to what you're looking for, then fill our the template below. Please read
     our guide to filing a bug first: https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports
-->
Thanks for awesome framework.
I've created an app with Flutter which targets Android TVs along with mobiles and tablets. With little tweaking the app works great on Android TVs too. I'm facing the focus issue of `TextFormField` specifically for Amazon FireTV device.

Here's minimalist reproducible example:

## Steps to Reproduce
<!-- You must include full steps to reproduce so that we can reproduce the problem. -->

1. Create a test app with `flutter create sandbox`.
2. Update the `main.dart` as follows: <!-- include every file that is different from the template app! -->
```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(MyApp());

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Sandbox',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: MyHomePage(),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  MyHomePage({Key key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  _MyHomePageState createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: Text('Sandbox App'),
      ),
      body: Align(
        alignment: Alignment.center,
        child: ConstrainedBox(
          constraints: BoxConstraints(
            maxWidth: 600.0,
          ),
          child: ListView(
            children: List<Widget>.generate(
              5,
              (int index) => TextFormField(
                decoration: InputDecoration(
                  labelText: 'Field $index',
                ),
                onEditingComplete: () => print("onEditingComplete"),
                onFieldSubmitted: (_) => print("onFieldSubmitted"),
                onSaved: (String value) => print("onSaved"),
              ),
            ),
          ),
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```
3. Run the app on Amazon FireTV device and enter text in input, then try to close the soft-keyboard using back button or previous button shown on keyboard itself.

**Expected results:** <!-- what did you want to see? -->
The soft-keyboard should close when back-button is pressed as it closes for Android native `EditText` view.

**Actual results:** <!-- what did you see? -->
On Android TV emulator it works fine. I'm able to enter text and when device back button is pressed the soft-keyboard closes as expected but for FireTV the soft-keyboard is shown as modal like view covering the input fields and when back-button is pressed, the "Previous" soft-keyboard button is highlighted but it never closes.

Here's GIF showing issue on FireTV:

![firetv_issue_resized](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/26060517/76145434-65b31580-60af-11ea-8c77-1ae16588cb70.gif)

Seems like the event is getting consumed by the `TextFormField`. Maybe we need to override `onKeyPreIme` as Firefox TV for FireTV does it.
https://github.com/mozilla-mobile/firefox-tv/blob/7ae0258756b348b82bd38330b22e6852e7468e82/app/src/main/java/org/mozilla/tv/firefox/widget/InlineAutocompleteEditText.java#L619

issues.title: KeyboardListener is buggy after focusing a TextField
issues.body:
This is primarily an issue for physical keyboards without touch screens but it is acting weird even with touch screens. I'm trying to support chromebooks with physical keyboard, that's why this issue is blocking me. I have not yet tested enough on slates with bluetooth keyboards but this may be a big problem with those devices too.

I posted this same question in a closed issue here but I didn't get any responses so I guess it's a new bug: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/11177

## Steps to Reproduce

I've created a simple repro: https://github.com/mikeesouth/chromebook_keyboard

This repro is as simple as this _(but my repro is slightly improved to show focus and removing unused code)_:

1. `flutter create chromebook_keyboard`
2. Add `final _keyboardListenerFocus = FocusNode();` to the state class
3. Add `String _keyName = 'N/A';` to the state class
4. Override dispose() and call `_keyboardListenerFocus.dispose();` in the state class
5. Add the following to the build method (e.g. as children to the column class):
```
Text('Here is plain TextField:'),
Container(width: 200, child: TextField()),
SizedBox(height: 50),
Text(
    'Tap the green container to show the keycode using a RawKeyboardListener:'),
GestureDetector(
  onTap: () => _keyboardListenerFocus.requestFocus(),
  child: Container(
    width: 200,
    height: 100,
    color: Colors.green,
    alignment: Alignment.center,
    child: RawKeyboardListener(
      focusNode: _keyboardListenerFocus,
      onKey: (keyEvent) => setState(
          () => _keyName = keyEvent.data.logicalKey.debugName),
      child: Text('keyName = $_keyName'),
    ),
  ),
),
```

**Expected results:**
I expect the physical keyboard to work for the TextField and the physical keyboard to also work for the RawKeyboardListener when pressing the green container (to set focus to the keyboard listener). I expect the RawKeyboardListener to register keys (e.g. "A") when it's focused.

**Actual results:**
The actual result is that the RawKeyboardListener listens correctly if I focus it before focusing the TextField after application startup. But as soon as I focus the TextField and then switch back the focus to the RawKeyboardListener - then it does not register keys correctly.
If you start with the green container (keyboard listener) it recognizes all keys (i.e a, b, c, 1, 2, 3, space, left alt, left shift and so on). If you switch to the text field you can type text as expected. If you switch back to the green container - it no longer recognizes text, only modifiers such as left alt, left shift etc. BUT if you hold left alt, then it also recognizes letters and other keys (i.e. alt + a will display "_keyName = A"). Restart the app - the KeyboardListener works again but as soon as you focus the TextField the RawKeyboardListener will no longer recognize key presses.

**Test device:**
I'm trying this on a Chromebook without touch screen and it (of course) has a physical keyboard. The model is `HP Chromebook 14-db0004no 14"`.

**Additional information - buggy on touch screens:**
Another interesting thing is that the keyboard listener correctly listens even after focusing the TextField. But an issue that may be related is that when I focus the TextField the soft keyboard shows as soon as I press a key on the physical keyboard - is this intended?

Steps to reproduce this issue that may be related:
1. Start the app
2. Tap the green container (to focus RawKeyboardListener)
3. Press some keys (e.g. a, b, c) on my physical keyboard (Windows) - it registers correctly and no soft keyboard is displayed
4. Tap the TextField (to focus it) - the soft keyboard pops up
5. Type some text (e.g. "Hello World")
6. Press back key on emulator to hide keyboard
7. Tap the green container again (to focus RawKeyboardListener)
8. Press some keys - the soft keyboard pops up again - why? _(side note: on the Android Emulator this still registers the keypresses, on my Chromebook, this wouldn't recognize the keypresses if I don't hold down alt as described above)_

I haven't started to test this on a hybrid device (i.e. touch screen + physical keyboard) but if the soft keyboard pops up focusing a text field when using a hybrid device with an attached keyboard, then it will become an issue for us. We would like to control when the soft keyboard shows up - it would be OK for it to show up automatically on a touch screen device without an attached keyboard.

I'm also running into this. I'm using a RawKeyboardListener to capture input from a barcode scanner on an Android device. The app also supports manual input via a Textfield. Once a Textfield has been focused, the soft keyboard keeps popping up when hardware keys are received, even is RawKeyboardListener is not used.

This seems to be resolved on master channel. Specifically that RawKeyboardListener does not register physical key presses after a TextField has been focused.

Can someone else confirm?

I'm experiencing this issue on macOS on the latest stable release (2.10.5). For me when I wrap a TextField in a RawKeyboardListener I'm unable to type normal Key characters (a,b,c,d, etc.). When I return the KeyEventResult.skipRemainingHandlers in OnKey I'm able to type but then keys like Backspace, Cmd+A for select all, etc don't work anymore. So I either get normal characters or the special keys but not both.

Edit: I was able to resolve my issue, which might be unrelated, by using the Shortcuts widget instead and in my Focus widget's `onKey` handler I returned `KeyEventResult.ignored` if my TextField's FocusNode was focused. My TextField now works as expected and my Shortcuts work when TextField's FocusNode isn't focused. I stumbled upon this issue while looking for a solution to implement keyboard shortcuts on desktop while also disabling the unhandled key sound on MacOS and needing global shortcuts, one being a shortcut to focus a search field and being able to unfocus it using the escape key.

A quick update from my side as the reporter of this issue. I see that others have the same (or related problems). I reported this issue in October 2020 with a very simple reproducible example: https://github.com/mikeesouth/chromebook_keyboard

The problem is still here in Flutter 3.3.2 and almost two years later. My repro can still be used to showcase the problem and I've just updated it to the latest Android project structure with updated gradle etc. The description that I wrote in this issue (first post above) is still valid and this problem still needs to be fixed in Flutter. I can reproduce this problem 100% on different devices, both Chromebooks, Android slates and Android phones. Both with Chromebook physical keyboard and no touch screen and on a Android phone with a connected bluetooth keyboard - same problem occurs. I'm not sure about iOS though.

The short version is: RawKeyboardListener works nicely but stops listening as soon as a text field is focused.

It does not matter if the text field is on the same page as the RawKeyboardListener. It doesn't matter if the RawKeyboardListener wraps the TextField or not. As soon as a text field is focused, the RawKeyboardListener stops working. Having an app with only TextFields (no RawKeyboardListeners) would work. Having an app with only RawKeyboardListeners and no TextFields would also work but when these are mixed - the problem occurs.

I would gladly take any advice on what I'm missing or doing wrong in the example app: https://github.com/mikeesouth/chromebook_keyboard
It can be solved by changing the input method, it is a bug of gboard
